---
title: Label reference
weight: 312
---

<!-- Code generated by cmd/labelsgen ("go generate ./..."); DO NOT EDIT. -->
<!-- Regenerate with `make labels-docs` after changing pkg/sablier/labels.go. -->

Sablier discovers and manages your workloads through these `sablier.*` labels. Each label is documented below with its type, the version it was introduced in, and any provider-specific behaviour. See the [compatibility matrix](/reference/compatibility/) for an at-a-glance provider grid.

## Applying labels {#applying-labels}

Attach the labels to the workload Sablier should manage. The mechanism differs per provider; the per-label notes below call out any provider-specific form.

{{< tabs >}}
{{< tab name="Docker" >}}
```yaml
services:
  whoami:
    image: containous/whoami
    labels:
      sablier.enable: "true"
      sablier.group: "my-group"
```
{{< /tab >}}
{{< tab name="Docker Swarm" >}}
```yaml
services:
  whoami:
    image: containous/whoami
    deploy:
      labels:
        sablier.enable: "true"
        sablier.group: "my-group"
```
{{< /tab >}}
{{< tab name="Kubernetes" >}}
```yaml
metadata:
  labels:
    sablier.enable: "true"      # discovery uses a label selector
  annotations:
    sablier.group: "my-group"   # all other settings are annotations
```
{{< /tab >}}
{{< tab name="Proxmox LXC" >}}
```bash
# Proxmox has no key/value labels, so Sablier reads container tags:
sablier                 # enables management (sablier.enable=true)
sablier-group-my-group  # one tag per group
```
{{< /tab >}}
{{< /tabs >}}

## Labels {#labels}

| Label | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`sablier.enable`](#label-sablier-enable) | Opts the instance into Sablier management. |
| [`sablier.group`](#label-sablier-group) | Assigns the instance to one or more named groups. |
| [`sablier.ready-after`](#label-sablier-ready-after) | The minimum settling delay after the instance first reports ready before Sablier treats it as ready. |
| [`sablier.ready-on-start`](#label-sablier-ready-on-start) | Treats the instance as ready as soon as the start is dispatched, skipping the health check. |
| [`sablier.running-hours`](#label-sablier-running-hours) | A daily keep-warm window in local time. |
| [`sablier.running-days`](#label-sablier-running-days) | Restricts the `sablier.running-hours` window to specific weekdays. |
| [`sablier.anti-affinity`](#label-sablier-anti-affinity) | Lists the group names this instance backs off from; it is forced idle while any listed group has an active session. |
| [`sablier.delegate-scaling`](#label-sablier-delegate-scaling) | Opts the instance into delegated scaling: Sablier never writes the replica count and instead emits "activate"/"deactivate" intent webhooks for an external scaler (e.g. a KEDA ScaledObject) to act on. |
| [`sablier.idle.replicas`](#label-sablier-idle-replicas) | The replica count when idle. |
| [`sablier.idle.cpu`](#label-sablier-idle-cpu) | The CPU limit applied when the session expires. |
| [`sablier.idle.memory`](#label-sablier-idle-memory) | The memory limit applied when the session expires. |
| [`sablier.active.replicas`](#label-sablier-active-replicas) | The replica count restored when a new session is requested. |
| [`sablier.active.cpu`](#label-sablier-active-cpu) | The CPU limit restored when a new session is requested. |
| [`sablier.active.memory`](#label-sablier-active-memory) | The memory limit restored when a new session is requested. |
| [`sablier.idle.blkio-weight`](#label-sablier-idle-blkio-weight) | The relative block I/O scheduling weight applied when idle. |
| [`sablier.active.blkio-weight`](#label-sablier-active-blkio-weight) | The relative block I/O scheduling weight restored when active. |
| [`sablier.idle.blkio-weight-device`](#label-sablier-idle-blkio-weight-device) | The per-device I/O scheduling weight applied when idle. |
| [`sablier.active.blkio-weight-device`](#label-sablier-active-blkio-weight-device) | The per-device I/O scheduling weight restored when active. |
| [`sablier.idle.blkio-device-read-bps`](#label-sablier-idle-blkio-device-read-bps) | The per-device read throughput limit applied when idle (Docker byte units, e.g. `10m` = 10 MB/s). |
| [`sablier.active.blkio-device-read-bps`](#label-sablier-active-blkio-device-read-bps) | The per-device read throughput limit restored when active. |
| [`sablier.idle.blkio-device-write-bps`](#label-sablier-idle-blkio-device-write-bps) | The per-device write throughput limit applied when idle. |
| [`sablier.active.blkio-device-write-bps`](#label-sablier-active-blkio-device-write-bps) | The per-device write throughput limit restored when active. |
| [`sablier.idle.blkio-device-read-iops`](#label-sablier-idle-blkio-device-read-iops) | The per-device read IOPS limit applied when idle. |
| [`sablier.active.blkio-device-read-iops`](#label-sablier-active-blkio-device-read-iops) | The per-device read IOPS limit restored when active. |
| [`sablier.idle.blkio-device-write-iops`](#label-sablier-idle-blkio-device-write-iops) | The per-device write IOPS limit applied when idle. |
| [`sablier.active.blkio-device-write-iops`](#label-sablier-active-blkio-device-write-iops) | The per-device write IOPS limit restored when active. |

### `sablier.enable` {#label-sablier-enable}

Opts the instance into Sablier management. Any value other than `true` is ignored.

{{< badge "boolean" >}} {{< badge content="Required" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.4.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.4.0" >}}

Example: `"true"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes must set this as a **label** (discovery uses a label selector), never an annotation. Proxmox LXC uses the `sablier` tag instead.
{{< /callout >}}

### `sablier.group` {#label-sablier-group}

Assigns the instance to one or more named groups. A session for any of its groups starts the instance.

{{< badge "comma-separated list" >}} {{< badge content="Default: \"default\"" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.4.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.4.0" >}}

Example: `"team-a,team-b"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes must set a multi-value list as an **annotation**. Proxmox LXC uses one `sablier-group-<name>` tag per group.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/groups/)

### `sablier.ready-after` {#label-sablier-ready-after}

The minimum settling delay after the instance first reports ready before Sablier treats it as ready.

{{< badge "Go duration" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.13.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0" >}}

Example: `"30s"`

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/readiness/ready-after/)

### `sablier.ready-on-start` {#label-sablier-ready-on-start}

Treats the instance as ready as soon as the start is dispatched, skipping the health check.

{{< badge "boolean" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"true"`

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/readiness/ready-on-start/)

### `sablier.running-hours` {#label-sablier-running-hours}

A daily keep-warm window in local time. Overnight windows like `22:00-06:00` span midnight.

{{< badge "`HH:MM-HH:MM`" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.13.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0" >}}

Example: `"09:00-18:00"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes must set the colon value as an **annotation**.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/lifecycle/running-hours/)

### `sablier.running-days` {#label-sablier-running-days}

Restricts the `sablier.running-hours` window to specific weekdays.

{{< badge "comma-separated weekdays" >}} {{< badge content="Default: every day" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"Mon,Tue,Wed,Thu,Fri"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes must set the comma value as an **annotation**.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/lifecycle/running-hours/)

### `sablier.anti-affinity` {#label-sablier-anti-affinity}

Lists the group names this instance backs off from; it is forced idle while any listed group has an active session.

{{< badge "comma-separated list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"streaming"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes must set a multi-value list as an **annotation**. Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/anti-affinity/)

### `sablier.delegate-scaling` {#label-sablier-delegate-scaling}

Opts the instance into delegated scaling: Sablier never writes the replica count and instead emits "activate"/"deactivate" intent webhooks for an external scaler (e.g. a KEDA ScaledObject) to act on.

{{< badge "boolean" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"true"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes only.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/delegated-scaling/)

### `sablier.idle.replicas` {#label-sablier-idle-replicas}

The replica count when idle. `0` stops the workload; `1` or more keeps it running with optional resource throttling.

{{< badge "integer" >}} {{< badge content="Default: `0` (stop)" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.13.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0" >}}

Example: `"1"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-mode/)

### `sablier.idle.cpu` {#label-sablier-idle-cpu}

The CPU limit applied when the session expires. Requires `sablier.idle.replicas >= 1`.

{{< badge "decimal cores / Kubernetes quantity" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.13.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0" >}}

Example: `"0.1"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes uses a resource quantity (e.g. `100m`). Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-cpu/)

### `sablier.idle.memory` {#label-sablier-idle-memory}

The memory limit applied when the session expires. Requires `sablier.idle.replicas >= 1`.

{{< badge "Docker units / Kubernetes quantity" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.13.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0" >}}

Example: `"128m"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes uses a resource quantity (e.g. `128Mi`). Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-memory/)

### `sablier.active.replicas` {#label-sablier-active-replicas}

The replica count restored when a new session is requested.

{{< badge "integer" >}} {{< badge content="Default: `1`" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.13.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0" >}}

Example: `"2"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-mode/)

### `sablier.active.cpu` {#label-sablier-active-cpu}

The CPU limit restored when a new session is requested.

{{< badge "decimal cores / Kubernetes quantity" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.13.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0" >}}

Example: `"2.0"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes uses a resource quantity (e.g. `2000m`). Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-cpu/)

### `sablier.active.memory` {#label-sablier-active-memory}

The memory limit restored when a new session is requested.

{{< badge "Docker units / Kubernetes quantity" >}} {{< badge content="Since v1.13.0" link="https://github.com/sablierapp/sablier/releases/tag/v1.13.0" >}}

Example: `"512m"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Kubernetes uses a resource quantity (e.g. `512Mi`). Not supported on Proxmox LXC.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-memory/)

### `sablier.idle.blkio-weight` {#label-sablier-idle-blkio-weight}

The relative block I/O scheduling weight applied when idle.

{{< badge "integer `10`-`1000`" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"100"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.active.blkio-weight` {#label-sablier-active-blkio-weight}

The relative block I/O scheduling weight restored when active.

{{< badge "integer `10`-`1000`" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"500"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.idle.blkio-weight-device` {#label-sablier-idle-blkio-weight-device}

The per-device I/O scheduling weight applied when idle.

{{< badge "`path:weight` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:100"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; per-device limits require daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.active.blkio-weight-device` {#label-sablier-active-blkio-weight-device}

The per-device I/O scheduling weight restored when active.

{{< badge "`path:weight` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:500"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; per-device limits require daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.idle.blkio-device-read-bps` {#label-sablier-idle-blkio-device-read-bps}

The per-device read throughput limit applied when idle (Docker byte units, e.g. `10m` = 10 MB/s).

{{< badge "`path:rate` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:10m"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.active.blkio-device-read-bps` {#label-sablier-active-blkio-device-read-bps}

The per-device read throughput limit restored when active.

{{< badge "`path:rate` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:100m"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.idle.blkio-device-write-bps` {#label-sablier-idle-blkio-device-write-bps}

The per-device write throughput limit applied when idle.

{{< badge "`path:rate` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:10m"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.active.blkio-device-write-bps` {#label-sablier-active-blkio-device-write-bps}

The per-device write throughput limit restored when active.

{{< badge "`path:rate` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:100m"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.idle.blkio-device-read-iops` {#label-sablier-idle-blkio-device-read-iops}

The per-device read IOPS limit applied when idle.

{{< badge "`path:iops` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:100"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.active.blkio-device-read-iops` {#label-sablier-active-blkio-device-read-iops}

The per-device read IOPS limit restored when active.

{{< badge "`path:iops` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:1000"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.idle.blkio-device-write-iops` {#label-sablier-idle-blkio-device-write-iops}

The per-device write IOPS limit applied when idle.

{{< badge "`path:iops` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:100"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)

### `sablier.active.blkio-device-write-iops` {#label-sablier-active-blkio-device-write-iops}

The per-device write IOPS limit restored when active.

{{< badge "`path:iops` list" >}} {{< badge content="Next release" >}}

Example: `"/dev/sda:1000"`

{{< callout type="info" >}}
Docker only; requires daemon API >= 1.55.
{{< /callout >}}

[Learn more](/how-to-guides/scaling-resources/scale-blkio/)
